PostToolUse.description: Validate epic closure prerequisites before marking epic as Done

hooks.prompt: If this update is marking an EPIC as Done (not a regular ticket), verify ALL of the following before allowing the closure: (1) All sub-tickets are Done or Cancelled. (2) No workarounds or temporary solutions were shipped. (3) Business value was delivered. (4) DEFERRAL JUSTIFICATION CHECK: For every sub-ticket comment containing a Deferred Items table, every row with classification `AC-DEFERRED` MUST be accompanied by a `### Deferral Justification (CATASTROPHIC — required)` block with all four fields populated and a catastrophic condition value in {1,2,3,4}. If any AC-DEFERRED entry lacks a valid justification block, BLOCK the epic closure and report the offending ticket(s) and item(s) — the user must either re-dispatch the deferred work or explicitly approve the deferral. (5) FOLLOW-UP CAP CHECK (≤3 absolute): If epic-closure is about to create more than 3 follow-up tickets total (the v4.6 absolute cap, replacing the v4.5 50% relative rule), BLOCK closure and require the user to either (a) apply the impact bar and closure-log discipline to fold items below the bar into a single `### Considered but not pursued` aggregation, or (b) explicitly override the cap with written justification per item. The cap is by design tight — sprawl reduction is the goal (PRO-1142 closure shipped with 1 follow-up vs PRO-793's 8 retrofits, an 8x reduction). See the `no-silent-deferrals`, `epic-closure-validation`, and `codex-finding-resolution` skills for the policy context.
